response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — including hands-on IDE development at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and authorship of test frameworks and runners — but the core required stack is largely absent. His professional depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are the central requirements for this enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE role.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored language tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Proven work on complex, large-scale systems, including low-latency services handling millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, and sustained open-source contributions
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only minor, dated professional Java exposure
No Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise JVM framework background
Recent career trajectory (full-stack web, pricing platforms, AI tooling) points away from JVM ecosystem depth
